message:
feat(layout,cli): extend layout algorithm and add CLI integration tests

Expand fm-layout with additional diagram layout improvements and extend
CLI integration test coverage. Sync beads tracker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3uz.26","title":"Release evidence artifact publishing, integrity checks, and retention policy","description":"Implement evidence artifact publishing and retention policy for release audits.

Scope:
- Publish traces, logs, metrics, drift reports, and proof artifacts with stable naming/versioning.
- Ensure release notes can link directly to evidence bundles.
- Enforce retention and integrity checks for auditability.

Verification:
- Integration tests for artifact completeness and link integrity.
- CI checks fail if required artifacts are missing or malformed.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:14:28.657425544Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:00.799700879Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["artifacts","audit","ci"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.26","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:14:28.657425544Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.26","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.25","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:14:29.356794921Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":80,"issue_id":"bd-3uz.26","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. - Require explicit approver identity, reason, scope, and expiration for overrides.
- Log override events with immutable audit metadata.
- Add post-override follow-up requirements (retro, fix bead creation, temporary exceptions expiry).

 {"id":"bd-3uz.3","title":"Add runtime capability metadata surfaces and docs auto-generation gate","description":"Expose runtime capability metadata and auto-generate support documentation from the same source of truth.

Scope:
- Add capability metadata endpoint/surface for CLI and WASM.
- Generate README support tables and docs snippets from `capability_matrix.json`.
- Add CI checks preventing manual docs drift from runtime capability metadata.
- Include per-feature confidence/support level and links to test evidence.

Why this matters:
Users should not need to guess which features are production-ready versus best-effort.

Acceptance criteria:
- CLI command and WASM API expose capability metadata.
- Docs generation is reproducible and CI-enforced.
- README support claims are 100% generated or validated against the matrix.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:34.123793197Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:02.100954296Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["automation","cli","docs","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:34.123793197Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:35.549941211Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:35.637874449Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":90,"issue_id":"bd-3uz.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. -{"id":"bd-3uz.4","title":"Implement staged LayoutAlgorithm dispatch framework with capability-aware selection ledger","description":"Implement a staged `LayoutAlgorithm` dispatch framework so algorithm selection is real, deterministic, and capability-aware even while some algorithms are still in-flight.

Scope:
- Implement deterministic dispatch and selection ledger for currently available algorithm families.
- Emit explicit `capability_unavailable` diagnostics when a requested algorithm family is not yet implemented.
- Add deterministic fallback mapping so user intent is preserved and never silently misrepresented.
- Keep selection criteria auditable with stable tie-breaks and trace fields.

Why this matters:
Dispatch logic should become correct and observable immediately; it should not wait on every downstream algorithm implementation bead.

Acceptance criteria:
- Dispatch framework is active for available families with deterministic selection logs.
- Unavailable families produce explicit diagnostics and deterministic fallback behavior.
- Unit/integration tests verify dispatch determinism, fallback correctness, and diagnostics stability.
- E2E runs include structured dispatch logs (requested vs selected algorithm, reason, fallback path, timings).","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:34.212977679Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:27:59.462231082Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["determinism","layout","telemetry"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:34.212977679Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.4","depends_on_id":"bd-vb9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:35.727242354Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":69,"issue_id":"bd-3uz.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. CI artifacts must be sufficient for post-mortem debugging without rerun.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:27:59Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3uz.5","title":"Implement native+WASM compute-pressure signal adapters with deterministic tier quantization","description":"Create cross-surface pressure signal adapters that estimate available compute budget without introducing nondeterministic behavior.

Scope:
- Native adapter: process-level timing/memory pressure heuristics.
- WASM adapter: frame budget / event-loop lag / worker saturation proxies.
- Normalize signals into deterministic, quantized pressure tiers consumed by the budget broker.
- Document signal limitations and fallback behavior when telemetry is unavailable.

Why this matters:
Graceful degradation requires pressure awareness, especially on overloaded machines.

Acceptance criteria:
- Pressure tier inputs available on CLI and WASM surfaces.
- Signal quantization is deterministic and replayable.
- Missing telemetry paths degrade safely with explicit diagnostics.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:34.304183819Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:01.974517837Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ops","runtime","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2u0.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:58:44.072941964Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:34.304183819Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":60,"issue_id":"bd-3uz.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Dependency note: pressure-signal adapters require baseline WASM API surface (`bd-2u0.3`) rather than completion of the entire web epic; this keeps pressure-resilience work from being artificially blocked.","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:58:44Z"},{"id":89,"issue_id":"bd-3uz.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. Required logs include input metadata, algorithm or mode selection, diagnostics, degradation or fallback decisions where applicable, timing metrics, output hashes or sizes, and explicit pass fail reasons. CI artifacts must be sufficient for post-mortem debugging without rerun.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:01Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3uz.6","title":"Implement cross-stage global budget broker for parse/layout/render","description":"Implement a global budget broker across parse/layout/render stages to enforce bounded runtime under pressure.

Scope:
- Allocate and enforce time/iteration/work budgets per stage.
- Expose deterministic arbitration policy when competing stages exceed budget.
- Integrate with layout time-budget guardrails and parser recovery budgets.
- Emit budget ledger events for observability and replay.

Why this matters:
Without a broker, stage-level safeguards are fragmented and overloaded runs can still destabilize.

Acceptance criteria:
- Hard budget enforcement exists across all core stages.
- Budget decisions are deterministic and logged.
- Synthetic worst-case corpus demonstrates bounded execution.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:34.390690384Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:27:59.337978232Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["guardrails","performance","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:34.390690384Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:36.218900928Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:36.305217236Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vb9.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:36.393120017Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":68,"issue_id":"bd-3uz.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. Required logs include input metadata, algorithm or mode selection, diagnostics, degradation or fallback decisions where applicable, timing metrics, output hashes or sizes, and explicit pass fail reasons. CI artifacts must be sufficient for post-mortem debugging without rerun.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:27:59Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3uz.7","title":"Create deterministic degradation-operator library for quality-vs-cost tradeoffs","description":"Build a deterministic degradation-operator library to reduce rendering/load cost while preserving structural correctness.

Scope:
- Operators: label elision, edge simplification, cluster collapse, routing simplification, animation/effect suppression.
- Define operator ordering and tie-break rules to preserve determinism.
- Ensure output remains semantically faithful and visibly marked as degraded where relevant.
- Add calibration knobs with safe defaults and reproducible behavior.

Why this matters:
Graceful degradation should be systematic, not ad hoc.

Acceptance criteria:
- Operator library integrated and test-covered.
- Degradation sequence is deterministic and replayable.
- Structural correctness invariants hold under all degradation tiers.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:34.480292207Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:27:59.194690822Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["degradation","layout","render"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:34.480292207Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:36.572620811Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:36.484452553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":67,"issue_id":"bd-3uz.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. @@ -363,7 +363,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-vb9.1","title":"Implement superior cycle handling in Sugiyama layout","description":"This is THE core differentiator mentioned in the X thread. Mermaid-js handles cycles poorly. Our approach:

1. DETECTION: Tarjan's SCC algorithm to find all strongly connected components. Report cycle count, participants, and nesting depth.

2. MULTI-STRATEGY CYCLE BREAKING (user-configurable):
   a. GREEDY (current): Source/sink peeling. Fast, good for mostly-DAG graphs.
   b. DFS-BACK: Depth-first search, reverse back edges. Minimizes reversed edges.
   c. MINIMUM-FEEDBACK-ARC-SET: Approximation algorithm (NP-hard exact). Finds near-minimal set of edges to reverse. Better aesthetics for heavily cyclic graphs.
   d. CYCLE-AWARE: Don't break cycles at all -- render them as visible cycles with curved back-edges. This is the 'honest' approach that shows the user their cycles rather than hiding them.

3. BACK-EDGE RENDERING: For reversed edges in GREEDY/DFS/MFAS modes, provide metadata so renderers can draw them distinctly (dashed line, different color, reversed arrowhead) to communicate that these represent cycles.

4. CYCLE CLUSTER COLLAPSE: Option to collapse an entire SCC into a single 'cycle cluster' node that can be expanded. Reduces visual complexity for highly cyclic subgraphs.

5. QUALITY METRICS: Track number of reversed edges, total edge length increase from reversal, visual symmetry of cycle representation. Expose in LayoutStats.

Determinism: All strategies must be deterministic given identical input. Use stable sorting, break ties by node ID.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All functionality explicitly described above is implemented fully; no scope reduction, no feature deletions, and no silent behavioral regressions.
- Code is modular, documented where needed, and integrated with existing crates/interfaces without compatibility shims or duplicated logic.
- Comprehensive automated tests are added/updated for this issue: focused unit tests + relevant integration coverage for touched behavior and edge cases.
- End-to-end validation is covered where applicable (CLI and/or WASM path) with detailed structured logging (inputs, detection results, node/edge counts, output sizes, timings, and pass/fail reasons).
- Diagnostics are actionable: failures/warnings include precise context and suggested remediation.
- Determinism and stability expectations are verified for affected outputs (layout/render/serialization as applicable).
- Quality gates pass: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo check --all-targets`, and relevant `cargo test` scope.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:31:50.539611823Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T01:33:49.344910966Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T01:33:49.343029732Z","close_reason":"Completed: added 12 comprehensive cycle strategy tests, SCC cycle cluster collapse with LayoutConfig/CycleClusterMap/LayoutCycleCluster, and edge length quality metrics (reversed_edge_total_length, total_edge_length). All 27 fm-layout tests pass, clippy clean across workspace.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cycles","differentiator","layout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vb9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3bc.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vb9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-vb9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-vb9.2","title":"Implement force-directed layout algorithm","description":"Implement a deterministic force-directed (spring-electric) layout for diagrams that don't have a natural hierarchy. Used for: ER diagrams (entities with many-to-many relationships), architecture diagrams, generic graphs with no clear flow direction.

Algorithm: Fruchterman-Reingold variant with:
1. Repulsive forces: All node pairs push apart (inverse-square law). Use Barnes-Hut approximation O(n log n) for graphs > 100 nodes.
2. Attractive forces: Connected nodes pull together (Hooke's law / log-spring).
3. Cooling schedule: Start with high temperature, reduce per iteration. Use deterministic schedule (no RNG -- use hash-based initial positions).
4. Convergence: Stop when max displacement < threshold or iteration budget exhausted.
5. Overlap removal: Post-processing pass to ensure no node overlap.
6. Cluster awareness: Nodes in same IrCluster get extra attractive force.

Key: Deterministic initial placement using hash of node IDs (not random). Same input always produces same output. Iteration count configurable via LayoutConfig.

Output: Same DiagramLayout type as Sugiyama. Callers don't need to know which algorithm was used.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All functionality explicitly described above is implemented fully; no scope reduction, no feature deletions, and no silent behavioral regressions.
- Code is modular, documented where needed, and integrated with existing crates/interfaces without compatibility shims or duplicated logic.
- Comprehensive automated tests are added/updated for this issue: focused unit tests + relevant integration coverage for touched behavior and edge cases.
- End-to-end validation is covered where applicable (CLI and/or WASM path) with detailed structured logging (inputs, detection results, node/edge counts, output sizes, timings, and pass/fail reasons).
- Diagnostics are actionable: failures/warnings include precise context and suggested remediation.
- Determinism and stability expectations are verified for affected outputs (layout/render/serialization as applicable).
- Quality gates pass: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo check --all-targets`, and relevant `cargo test` scope.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:32:25.554773182Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T02:10:58.311427014Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T02:10:58.311404351Z","close_reason":"Implemented Fruchterman-Reingold force-directed layout: deterministic hash-based initial placement, O(n^2) repulsive forces with Barnes-Hut grid approximation for n>100, Hooke's law attractive forces, linear cooling schedule, convergence detection, iterative overlap removal, cluster-aware cohesion forces, straight-line edge routing with rect-border clipping. 15 new tests (42 total). Clippy clean across workspace.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["algorithm","layout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vb9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3bc.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vb9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-vb9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-vb9.3","title":"Implement tree and radial layout algorithms","description":"Implement Reingold-Tilford tree layout and radial variant for hierarchical diagrams. Used for: mindmaps, org charts, file trees, decision trees.

Tree layout (Reingold-Tilford):
1. Post-order traversal to compute subtree widths
2. Assign x-coordinates using minimum-width algorithm with contour merging
3. Pre-order traversal to assign final positions
4. Support for multiple root nodes (forest layout)
5. Configurable: top-down, bottom-up, left-right, right-left orientations

Radial layout variant:
1. Root at center, children on concentric rings
2. Angular spread proportional to subtree size
3. Avoid label overlap with angular separation constraints
4. Used specifically for mindmap diagrams

Both must handle: variable node sizes, edge labels, cluster boundaries. Output: standard DiagramLayout.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All functionality explicitly described above is implemented fully; no scope reduction, no feature deletions, and no silent behavioral regressions.
- Code is modular, documented where needed, and integrated with existing crates/interfaces without compatibility shims or duplicated logic.
- Comprehensive automated tests are added/updated for this issue: focused unit tests + relevant integration coverage for touched behavior and edge cases.
- End-to-end validation is covered where applicable (CLI and/or WASM path) with detailed structured logging (inputs, detection results, node/edge counts, output sizes, timings, and pass/fail reasons).
- Diagnostics are actionable: failures/warnings include precise context and suggested remediation.
- Determinism and stability expectations are verified for affected outputs (layout/render/serialization as applicable).
- Quality gates pass: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo check --all-targets`, and relevant `cargo test` scope.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:32:25.633366659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T04:29:17.102581261Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T04:29:17.102550754Z","close_reason":"Implemented deterministic tree and radial layout algorithms with orientation support, forest handling, angular spread, and tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["algorithm","layout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vb9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3bc.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vb9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vb9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-vb9.4","title":"Implement specialized layouts for timeline, gantt, sankey, kanban, grid","description":"Implement purpose-built layout algorithms for diagram types that don't fit general graph layout:

1. TIMELINE LAYOUT: Linear horizontal arrangement. Time periods evenly spaced. Events stacked vertically under each period. Sections as labeled bands. Simple arithmetic positioning.

2. GANTT LAYOUT: Horizontal bar chart on time axis. Tasks as bars with start/end. Dependencies as arrows between bars. Critical path highlighting. Sections as swim lanes. Calendar grid background.

3. SANKEY LAYOUT: Flow-conserving layout. Nodes in columns, edges as variable-width bands. Uses iterative relaxation to minimize edge crossings while maintaining flow conservation (inspired by d3-sankey algorithm). Node heights proportional to flow.

4. KANBAN LAYOUT: Fixed columns (swim lanes). Cards stacked vertically in columns. WIP limit indicators. Simple grid-based positioning.

5. GRID/BLOCK LAYOUT: CSS-grid-like positioning with column/row spans. Used for block diagrams. Cells sized by content, spans merged.

6. PIE/RADAR LAYOUT: Polar coordinate systems. Pie: sectors by proportion. Radar: axes from center, data points on axes connected by polygons.

Each layout produces DiagramLayout with same interface as Sugiyama/force-directed. Specialized metadata (e.g. time axis ticks for gantt) stored in layout extensions.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All functionality explicitly described above is implemented fully; no scope reduction, no feature deletions, and no silent behavioral regressions.
- Code is modular, documented where needed, and integrated with existing crates/interfaces without compatibility shims or duplicated logic.
- Comprehensive automated tests are added/updated for this issue: focused unit tests + relevant integration coverage for touched behavior and edge cases.
- End-to-end validation is covered where applicable (CLI and/or WASM path) with detailed structured logging (inputs, detection results, node/edge counts, output sizes, timings, and pass/fail reasons).
- Diagnostics are actionable: failures/warnings include precise context and suggested remediation.
- Determinism and stability expectations are verified for affected outputs (layout/render/serialization as applicable).

+{"id":"bd-vb9.4","title":"Implement specialized layouts for timeline, gantt, sankey, kanban, grid","description":"Implement purpose-built layout algorithms for diagram types that don't fit general graph layout:

1. TIMELINE LAYOUT: Linear horizontal arrangement. Time periods evenly spaced. Events stacked vertically under each period. Sections as labeled bands. Simple arithmetic positioning.

2. GANTT LAYOUT: Horizontal bar chart on time axis. Tasks as bars with start/end. Dependencies as arrows between bars. Critical path highlighting. Sections as swim lanes. Calendar grid background.

3. SANKEY LAYOUT: Flow-conserving layout. Nodes in columns, edges as variable-width bands. Uses iterative relaxation to minimize edge crossings while maintaining flow conservation (inspired by d3-sankey algorithm). Node heights proportional to flow.

4. KANBAN LAYOUT: Fixed columns (swim lanes). Cards stacked vertically in columns. WIP limit indicators. Simple grid-based positioning.

5. GRID/BLOCK LAYOUT: CSS-grid-like positioning with column/row spans. Used for block diagrams. Cells sized by content, spans merged.

6. PIE/RADAR LAYOUT: Polar coordinate systems. Pie: sectors by proportion. Radar: axes from center, data points on axes connected by polygons.

Each layout produces DiagramLayout with same interface as Sugiyama/force-directed. Specialized metadata (e.g. time axis ticks for gantt) stored in layout extensions.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All functionality explicitly described above is implemented fully; no scope reduction, no feature deletions, and no silent behavioral regressions.
- Code is modular, documented where needed, and integrated with existing crates/interfaces without compatibility shims or duplicated logic.
- Comprehensive automated tests are added/updated for this issue: focused unit tests + relevant integration coverage for touched behavior and edge cases.
- End-to-end validation is covered where applicable (CLI and/or WASM path) with detailed structured logging (inputs, detection results, node/edge counts, output sizes, timings, and pass/fail reasons).
- Diagnostics are actionable: failures/warnings include precise context and suggested remediation.
- Determinism and stability expectations are verified for affected outputs (layout/render/serialization as applicable).
- Quality gates pass: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo check --all-targets`, and relevant `cargo test` scope.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:32:25.710722783Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T20:55:49.333307299Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["algorithm","layout","specialized"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vb9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3bc.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vb9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-vb9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-vb9.5","title":"Improve crossing minimization with advanced heuristics","description":"Upgrade the barycenter crossing minimization in the Sugiyama pipeline with more sophisticated heuristics for better edge routing aesthetics:

1. SIFTING: After barycenter, apply sifting heuristic -- try moving each node to every position in its layer, keep the position with fewest crossings. O(n^2) per layer but significant quality improvement.

2. TRANSPOSE: Pairwise swapping of adjacent nodes if it reduces crossings. Iterate until no improvement.

3. GLOBAL SIFTING: Move nodes across layers (re-rank) if it reduces total crossings. Bounded to prevent infinite loops.

4. EDGE BUNDLING: Group edges with similar source/target layers and route them through shared waypoints. Reduces visual clutter for dense graphs.

5. LONG EDGE OPTIMIZATION: For edges spanning multiple ranks, ensure dummy nodes are well-positioned to minimize bends. Current implementation uses median; try also left-right centering.

6. PORT ORDERING: When a node has multiple ports, order them to minimize crossings of attached edges. Currently ports are arbitrary; this matters for class/ER diagrams.

Quality metric: track crossing count before/after each heuristic. Expose in LayoutStats so users can see improvement.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All functionality explicitly described above is implemented fully; no scope reduction, no feature deletions, and no silent behavioral regressions.

- Diagnostics are actionable: failures/warnings include precise context and suggested remediation.

- Quality gates pass: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo check --all-targets`, and relevant `cargo test` scope.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:32:25.830257844Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T02:18:20.421996886Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T02:18:20.421974013Z","close_reason":"Implemented transpose and sifting crossing refinement heuristics. Transpose: iteratively swaps adjacent nodes in each rank if it reduces crossings (up to 10 passes). Sifting: tries every position for each node in its rank, keeps the one with fewest crossings. Added crossing_count_before_refinement to LayoutStats. Added crossing_refinement trace stage. 6 new tests (48 total). Clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["algorithm","layout","polish"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vb9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3bc.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vb9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vb9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-vb9.6","title":"Implement orthogonal edge routing with channel assignment","description":"Implement proper orthogonal (Manhattan-style) edge routing that produces clean, non-overlapping edge paths:

1. CHANNEL ASSIGNMENT: Between each pair of adjacent ranks, assign horizontal routing channels. Edges that need to turn are assigned to channels to avoid overlap.

2. NUDGE ALGORITHM: After initial routing, nudge edge segments to reduce total edge length and improve spacing.

3. CONFLICT RESOLUTION: When edges must cross, minimize crossing angle and ensure crossing point is visually clear (not on a bend).

4. POLYLINE SIMPLIFICATION: Remove unnecessary waypoints from edge paths. If three consecutive waypoints are collinear, remove the middle one.

5. SPLINE OPTION: As alternative to orthogonal, offer cubic Bezier spline routing. Control points computed from waypoint geometry for smooth curves. Configurable per-edge or globally.

6. SELF-LOOP ROUTING: Special handling for edges where source == target. Route as a loop going out the right/bottom and returning to left/top.

7. PARALLEL EDGE ROUTING: When multiple edges connect the same pair of nodes, offset them to be visually distinguishable.

This significantly improves visual quality over the current waypoint-based routing.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All functionality explicitly described above is implemented fully; no scope reduction, no feature deletions, and no silent behavioral regressions.

 {"id":"bd-vb9.7","title":"Implement automatic layout algorithm selection","description":"Create an intelligent algorithm selector that chooses the best layout algorithm based on diagram characteristics:

1. DiagramType mapping: flowchart/class/state -> Sugiyama, ER -> force-directed, mindmap -> radial-tree, gantt -> gantt-specific, etc.

2. Graph metrics analysis: For ambiguous types, analyze graph structure:
   - High ratio of edges to nodes (>2:1) -> force-directed (too many crossings for Sugiyama)
   - Mostly tree-like (few back edges) -> tree layout
   - Many SCCs -> cycle-aware Sugiyama
   - Very sparse -> force-directed (Sugiyama wastes space)

3. Override: Users can force a specific algorithm via config. Auto-selection is the default.

4. Fallback chain: If selected algorithm fails (e.g., timeout), fall back to simpler algorithm. Sugiyama -> force-directed -> simple grid.

Output: LayoutAlgorithm enum + selection rationale string for debugging.
